title: carex canescens subsp. lapponica
intro: Carex canescens subsp. lapponica, also known as the pale sedge or Laponian pale sedge, is a subspecies of sedge plant that belongs to the genus Carex. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems due to its antiseptic and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Carex canescens subsp. lapponica include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. Carex canescens subsp. lapponica is typically prepared for medicinal use through infusion or decoction of its leaves and roots, and it can also be found in herbal supplements and teas.
